name: Labrador Retriever
description: The Labrador Retriever is the most popular dog breed in the US, UK, and Canada. Friendly, active, and outgoing, Labs excel as family companions and working dogs. As a large breed, they are prone to hip and elbow dysplasia, obesity, and exercise-induced collapse (EIC). Routine orthopedic screening and weight management are essential.

name: German Shepherd Dog
description: The German Shepherd Dog is a large, agile, and muscular working breed known for intelligence, loyalty, and versatility. GSDs serve as police, military, guide, and search-and-rescue dogs worldwide. Health concerns center on hip and elbow dysplasia, degenerative myelopathy (DM), and bloat. Lifetime orthopedic monitoring is critical.

name: Dachshund
description: The Dachshund, known as the 'wiener dog', is a small hound with a long body and short legs. Standard and miniature sizes exist, across smooth, wirehaired, and longhaired coat types. The breed's elongated spine makes Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD) the primary health crisis — approximately 25% of Dachshunds are affected. Weight control and avoiding stairs/jumping are critical management strategies.

name: Poodle
description: Poodles come in three size varieties — Standard, Miniature, and Toy — but share a curly, hypoallergenic coat and exceptional intelligence. The breed is prone to Addison's disease, sebaceous adenitis (SA), hip dysplasia, and progressive retinal atrophy. Each size variety has distinct health profiles requiring tailored screening.

name: English Bulldog
description: The English Bulldog is a muscular, heavy-boned breed with a distinctive wrinkled face and pushed-in nose. While beloved for their gentle, courageous temperament, Bulldogs face severe brachycephalic health challenges, including BOAS, skin fold infections, and heat intolerance. They are also prone to hip dysplasia, cherry eye, and cardiac issues. Daily wrinkle care and respiratory monitoring are non-negotiable.

name: Boxer
description: The Boxer is a medium-to-large working breed known for boundless energy, loyalty, and a distinctive square-jawed head. Boxers are predisposed to several cancers including lymphoma and mast cell tumors, as well as dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM), aortic stenosis, and hip dysplasia. Cancer is the leading cause of death in Boxers. Regular cardiac and oncology screening is recommended.

name: Beagle
description: The Beagle is a small-to-medium scent hound with a friendly, curious nature. Originally bred for hunting hare, Beagles have an extraordinary sense of smell. Common health concerns include epilepsy, hypothyroidism, intervertebral disc disease, and chronic ear infections (due to long floppy ears). Cherry eye and glaucoma are ocular concerns.

name: Rottweiler
description: The Rottweiler is a large, powerful working breed descended from Roman drover dogs. Confident and loyal, Rottweilers excel in protection, herding, and service work. The breed faces elevated risks of osteosarcoma (bone cancer), hip and elbow dysplasia, and aortic stenosis. Cancer — particularly osteosarcoma — is the leading cause of death in Rottweilers.

name: Persian
description: The Persian is a long-haired cat breed with a distinctive round face, short muzzle, and calm, gentle personality. One of the oldest and most popular purebred cats. Persians are predisposed to Polycystic Kidney Disease (PKD) — an autosomal dominant condition affecting up to 40% of the breed — as well as hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and progressive retinal atrophy. Brachycephalic facial structure causes respiratory and dental crowding issues.

name: Siamese
description: The Siamese is one of the most recognizable cat breeds, known for striking blue almond-shaped eyes, color-pointed coat, and extremely vocal, social personality. Siamese cats form strong bonds with their owners. Common health concerns include progressive retinal atrophy (PRA), asthma, dental disease, and amyloidosis (a liver disease). They are also prone to separation anxiety when left alone.
